title: Import Jerboa Prelude (Basic Pattern)
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; Now you have: def, defstruct, defclass, defmethod, match, try/catch,
; hash, sort, format, JSON, path, string, list, alist operations
(def (greet name)
  (format "Hello, ~a!" name))

(display (greet "World"))
(newline)
notes: The jerboa prelude provides Gerbil-style syntax on top of Chez Scheme. Import it first in any Jerboa file.

title: Define and Use Structs
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

(defstruct Point (x y))

(def p (make-Point 3 4))
(display (Point-x p))  ; => 3
(display (Point? p))   ; => #t

; With default values (if supported)
(defstruct Config
  (host "localhost")
  (port 8080))

(def cfg (make-Config))
(display (Config-port cfg))  ; => 8080
notes: defstruct generates make-NAME, NAME?, and NAME-FIELD accessors.

title: Hash Table Creation and Access
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; Create hash table
(def h (hash 'name "Alice" 'age 30))

; Access (returns #!void if not found)
(hash-ref h 'name)     ; => "Alice"

; Safe access with default
(hash-get h 'email #f)  ; => #f (key not found)

; Modify (hash tables are mutable)
(hash-set! h 'email "alice@example.com")

; Iterate
(hash-for-each h
  (lambda (k v)
    (format #t "~a: ~a\n" k v)))

; Check membership
(hash-ref? h 'name)  ; => #t
notes: Use hash-get instead of hash-ref when key may be missing. hash-ref returns #!void for missing keys.

title: Pattern Matching
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; Basic match
(def (describe x)
  (match x
    [0 "zero"]
    [(? number? n) (format "number ~a" n)]
    [(? string? s) (format "string: ~a" s)]
    [(a b) (format "pair: ~a ~a" a b)]
    [(a . rest) (format "list starting with ~a" a)]
    [_ "other"]))

(display (describe 42))    ; => "number 42"
(display (describe "hi"))  ; => "string: hi"
(display (describe '(1 2 3))) ; => "list starting with 1"
notes: match is from the jerboa prelude. Patterns include literals, predicates with ?, list patterns, and wildcards _.

title: Error Handling with try/catch
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; try/catch style (from jerboa prelude)
(try
  (error "something went wrong" 42)
  (catch e
    (display (condition/message e))
    (newline)))

; guard style (R6RS/Chez)
(guard (e
        [(&error? e) (condition/message e)]
        [else "unknown error"])
  (error "test error"))

; Re-raise
(guard (e
        [(string-prefix? "expected" (condition/message e))
         (raise e)]   ; re-raise
        [else #f])   ; suppress others
  ...)
notes: Both try/catch (jerboa prelude) and guard (R6RS) work. guard is more flexible for matching condition types.

title: JSON Parsing
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))
(import (std text json))

; Parse JSON string
(def data (json-read (open-string-input-port "{\"name\": \"Alice\", \"age\": 30}")))

; Access fields (returns hash table)
(hash-ref data "name")  ; => "Alice"
(hash-ref data "age")   ; => 30

; Parse JSON array
(def arr (json-read (open-string-input-port "[1, 2, 3]")))
; arr is a vector: #(1 2 3)

; Read from file
(call-with-input-file "data.json"
  (lambda (port)
    (json-read port)))
notes: json-read returns hash tables for objects, vectors for arrays. Strings use Scheme strings.

title: Sorting Lists
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; Basic sort (sort is in prelude)
(sort '(3 1 4 1 5 9 2 6) <)  ; => (1 1 2 3 4 5 6 9)

; Sort strings
(sort '("banana" "apple" "cherry") string<?)

; Sort by key
(def people '((alice 30) (bob 25) (carol 35)))
(sort people (lambda (a b) (< (cadr a) (cadr b))))
; => ((bob 25) (alice 30) (carol 35))

; Sort with std sort module (more options)
(import (std sort))
(sort! '(3 1 2) <)  ; destructive sort
notes: sort is included in the jerboa prelude. For destructive sort!, use (import (std sort)).

title: File I/O
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; Read entire file
(def contents
  (call-with-input-file "data.txt"
    (lambda (port)
      (let loop ([lines '()])
        (def line (read-line port))
        (if (eof-object? line)
            (reverse lines)
            (loop (cons line lines)))))))

; Write to file
(call-with-output-file "output.txt"
  (lambda (port)
    (display "hello" port)
    (newline port)))

; Read entire file as string (with std module)
(import (std misc ports))
(def text (read-all-text "data.txt"))
notes: Chez file I/O uses call-with-input-file/call-with-output-file. std misc ports provides read-all-text.

title: Writing Tests
code:
(import (std test))

; Define a test suite
(test-suite "my-module tests"
  
  (test "basic arithmetic"
    (check (+ 1 2) => 3)
    (check (- 10 3) => 7))
  
  (test "string operations"
    (check (string-length "hello") => 5)
    (check (string-append "foo" "bar") => "foobar"))
  
  (test "list operations"
    (check (length '(1 2 3)) => 3)
    (check (car '(1 2 3)) => 1)))

; Run with: scheme --libdirs $JERBOA_HOME/lib --script test.ss
notes: Tests use (std test) module. check compares with =>, string= for strings, equal? for structures.

title: Running a Jerboa Script
code:
#!/usr/bin/env scheme --libdirs ${JERBOA_HOME:-~/mine/jerboa}/lib --script

; Or run manually:
; scheme --libdirs $JERBOA_HOME/lib --script myscript.ss

; Compile imported libraries for speed:
; scheme --compile-imported-libraries --libdirs $JERBOA_HOME/lib --script myscript.ss

(import (jerboa prelude))

; Command-line args
(def args (cdr (command-line)))

(display "Hello from Jerboa!")
(newline)
notes: Set JERBOA_HOME to point to your Jerboa installation. The --compile-imported-libraries flag pre-compiles .sls to .so for faster startup.

title: Keyword Argument Functions
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; Function with keyword arguments
(def (connect host: (host "localhost")
              port: (port 5432)
              db: db)
  (format "connecting to ~a:~a/~a" host port db))

; Call with keyword args
(connect db: "mydb")              ; uses defaults
(connect host: "db.example.com"
         port: 5433
         db: "prod")

; Mixed positional and keyword
(def (process items limit: (limit 100))
  (take items limit))

(process '(1 2 3 4 5) limit: 3)   ; => (1 2 3)
notes: Keyword args use the keyword: (default) syntax from jerboa prelude. Caller uses name: value.

title: Path Manipulation
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))
(import (std os path))

; Basic path ops
(path-join "/home" "user" "file.txt")  ; => "/home/user/file.txt"
(path-basename "/home/user/file.txt")  ; => "file.txt"
(path-dirname "/home/user/file.txt")   ; => "/home/user"
(path-extension "file.txt")            ; => ".txt"

; Check existence
(file-exists? "/etc/hosts")   ; => #t
(directory? "/tmp")           ; => #t

; List directory
(directory-list "/tmp")       ; list of filenames

; Expand ~
(path-expand "~/mine/jerboa") ; expands ~
notes: Path operations from (std os path). Use path-join for portable path construction.

title: Channel-Based Concurrency
code:
(import (std misc channel))
(import (std misc threads))

; Worker thread pattern
(def work-ch (make-channel))
(def result-ch (make-channel))

(def worker
  (thread-start!
    (make-thread
      (lambda ()
        (let loop ()
          (def job (channel-receive work-ch))
          (if (eq? job 'done)
              (channel-send result-ch 'finished)
              (begin
                (channel-send result-ch (* job job)) ; square it
                (loop))))))))

; Send work
(channel-send work-ch 5)     ; => result-ch gets 25
(def result (channel-receive result-ch))
(display result)  ; => 25

(channel-send work-ch 'done)
(thread-join! worker)
notes: Use channels for safe communication between threads. channel-send blocks until receiver is ready.

title: Gerbil to Jerboa Import Translation
code:
; Gerbil imports -> Jerboa equivalents

; Gerbil:              Jerboa:
; (import :std/sort)   (import (std sort))
; (import :std/text/json) (import (std text json))
; (import :gerbil/gambit) [NOT AVAILABLE - use Chez builtins]

; Gerbil syntax:       Jerboa equivalent:
; (import :std/misc/hash) -- hash ops are in (jerboa prelude)
; with-catch           -- use guard or try/catch from prelude
; (##...) primitives   -- NOT available, use Chez R6RS equivalents
; (export #t)          -- must enumerate exports explicitly

; Common mappings:
; Gerbil              -> Jerboa
; hash-table-ref      -> hash-ref
; hash-table-set!     -> hash-set!
; hash-table-for-each -> hash-for-each
; make-hash-table     -> (hash) or make-equal-hashtable
notes: When porting from Gerbil, the biggest changes are import paths (: prefix -> parens), no (export #t), and no ## Gambit primitives.

title: Custom Syntax with defrule
code:
(import (jerboa prelude))

; Simple defrule macro
(defrule (swap! a b)
  (let ([tmp a])
    (set! a b)
    (set! b tmp)))

; Usage
(define x 1)
(define y 2)
(swap! x y)
; x = 2, y = 1

; More complex pattern
(defrule (while condition body ...)
  (let loop ()
    (when condition
      body ...
      (loop))))

; Usage
(define i 0)
(while (< i 5)
  (display i)
  (set! i (+ i 1)))
notes: defrule is from (jerboa prelude) and is sugar over define-syntax + syntax-rules. It's hygienic by default.
